Comparative-Analysis.title: Comparative Analysis
Comparative-Analysis.content:
“How can we provide personalized user experiences in shared mobility services using SDV and AI technologies in a fully autonomous (Level 5) future?”

In the future, car ownership is expected to decrease, and autonomous shared mobility, where strangers share rides like public transportation will become more common.
If you own a car, it’s completely yours, so you can personalize it to your taste, like decorating the interior or listening to your favorite music.
But with shared mobility, that kind of personalization isn’t really possible. So I thought what if we could use SDVs(Software Defined Vehicles) and AI technology to create personalized experiences within shared mobility services?

Expected Output: Mobile UXUI + In-car experience(infotainment system)

This study compares two distinct layers of the user journey:
the in-car experience system, represented by M.VICS 5.0, Tesla Model 3 (2025), and MBUX;
the service-level experience (MaaS), represented by Uber;
and an integrated shared mobility platform, represented by MOIA, combining both in-vehicle experience and service-level integration.
Five criteria: Context Awareness, Multi-User Handling, Interface Adaptability, AI Integration, Ambient Experience Design

Question!
Q. Do I need to separate MaaS and in-car experience? The reason I combine them is that users perceive their experience as a continuous journey from booking to boarding, during the ride, and even after getting off.
Q. “For each column (product), highlight the text of one cell in red that you believe is most important to that product’s success – you should have only one red cell per column.” for the concept, it’s hard to see the success.. Is it mandatory?

Future-Scenario-Development-(WIP).title: Future Scenario Development (WIP)
Future-Scenario-Development-(WIP).content:
3.4.1 Urban and Technological Context (2040)
This study assumes a society in the year 2040 where fully autonomous vehicles are widely used, and shared mobility services are a natural part of everyday urban life.
This future scenario is based on expert predictions and industry reports. According to McKinsey & Company (2021), while Level 5 autonomy could become technically achievable in the early 2030s, broader adoption may take longer due to infrastructure, legal regulations, and societal acceptance. Similarly, the Boston Consulting Group (2021) expects that fully autonomous vehicles will become commercially viable between 2035 and 2040, with earlier adoption likely to happen in urban areas.
3.4.2 User Persona
Ella is a 32-year-old management consultant living in Singapore.
Singapore was selected as the persona’s setting for 2040 due to its active promotion of autonomous mobility and its urban development strategies aimed at reducing private car ownership. The city has designated several areas as testbeds for self-driving vehicles and continues to expand its “car-lite” zones and on-demand shuttle systems through government-led initiatives (ITS International, 2021; Hankyung, 2023).
Although Singapore has a comprehensive public transportation system, Ella prefers not to rely on it. She finds frequent transfers and crowded conditions inefficient, especially when she needs to focus on work during her commute. These factors suggest a future where autonomous and on-demand mobility services are a preferred option for professionals with similar needs.
Ella follows a hybrid work schedule and commutes to the office three days a week. To improve the efficiency and comfort of her daily travel, she chose not to own a car and instead subscribed to a shared autonomous mobility service. She has been using this service for the past six months, mainly for commuting, but also for social and professional appointments across the city.
For Ella, time and concentration are important. She often uses her commuting time to review presentations, respond to messages, or enjoy a quiet moment before work. The private and adaptive environment of the autonomous vehicle helps her manage her time more effectively and transition smoothly between work and personal life, avoiding the unpredictability often associated with public transport.
3.4.3 Future Mobility Scenario

index.title: 🚘 The Future of Shared Mobility: From Seamless Integration to Personalized Experiences
index.content:
With the advancement of technologies, the mobility industry has been rapidly shifting from ‘transportation-focused’ to ‘mobility-focused’. In the past, the focus was mainly on designing and developing specific modes of transportation, such as cars, trains, and buses, as well as operating them efficiently. However, more recently, the focus has shifted to the overall experience of mobility, along with innovations in technologies and services. This shift has been driven by advancements of innovative technologies such as AVs (Autonomous Vehicles), SDVs (Software Defined Vehicles), and MaaS (Mobility as a Service). These technologies go beyond simply providing means of transportation; they are becoming more integrated into our daily lives, creating personalized and efficient mobility experiences.
Aligned with this shift, this project will explore potential future scenarios in mobility to envision new possibilities. These are the key aspects I aim to research and design:

How shared mobility can seamlessly integrate into our daily lives.
How SDV (Software Defined Vehicle) technology combined with AI can deliver personalized experiences in shared mobility.
